name: G1→G2 Zone 3 — The Glacial Approach Wandering Monsters
description: Wandering monster table for Zone 3 (Days 5–6): Glacier, crevasse fields, permanent ice. G2 territory. The Rift is visible. Check frequency: once per 8 hours of travel, once per 8 hours of rest.

results.text: 1d4 Frost Giants (perimeter patrol from the Rift; alert) [PF2e Lvl 9 each]
2d4 Winter Wolves (frost giant hunting pack, off-leash) [PF2e Lvl 5 each]
1 Frost Giant Jarl's Guard (elite patrol) [PF2e Lvl 11]
1d4 Woolly Mammoths (glacial herd; not hostile unless cornered) [PF2e Lvl 7 each]
1 Remorhaz, Adult (hunts the crevasse fields) [PF2e Lvl 10]
2d6 Bugbears (serving the Jarl; armed with G2-made cold-iron weapons) [PF2e Lvl 3 each]
1 Young Adult White Dragon (serves the Jarl; will attempt to report the party) [PF2e Lvl 10]
2d6 Goblins (G2 scouts; will flee and report rather than fight) [PF2e Lvl –1 each]
1d4 Frost Giant Sub-Chiefs (returning from a raid; loaded with loot) [PF2e Lvl 10 each]
HAZARD — Crevasse: DC 20 Perception to spot; failure means 1d4 members fall (DC 18 Reflex) — depth 2d8 × 10 feet
1 Adult White Dragon (senior; guards the outer glacier approaches to the Rift) [PF2e Lvl 12 — very dangerous]
2d4 Frost Giant Scouts on captured hippogriffs (aerial patrol; advantage on Perception) [PF2e Lvl 9 each]
HAZARD — Glacial Calving: a section of the glacier breaks away; DC 22 Reflex or swept 60 feet and 6d6 bludgeoning; creates impassable terrain
